verses.sanskrit: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाचइन्द्रमेवं समादिश्य भगवान् विश्वभावन: ।पश्यतामनिमेषाणां तत्रैवान्तर्दधे हरि: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvācaindram evaṁ samādiśyabhagavān viśva-bhāvanaḥpaśyatām animeṣāṇāṁtatraivāntardadhe hariḥ
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: After instructing Indra in this way, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Hari, the cause of the cosmic manifestation, then and there disappeared from the presence of the onlooking demigods.

verses.sanskrit: तथाभियाचितो देवैर्ऋषिराथर्वणो महान् ।मोदमान उवाचेदं प्रहसन्निव भारत ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathābhiyācito devairṛṣir ātharvaṇo mahānmodamāna uvācedaṁprahasann iva bhārata
verses.text: O King Parīkṣit, following the Lord’s instructions, the demigods approached Dadhīci, the son of Atharvā. He was very liberal, and when they begged him to give them his body, he at once partially agreed. However, just to hear religious instructions from them, he smiled and jokingly spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: अपि वृन्दारका यूयं न जानीथ शरीरिणाम् ।संस्थायां यस्त्वभिद्रोहो दु:सहश्चेतनापह: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: api vṛndārakā yūyaṁna jānītha śarīriṇāmsaṁsthāyāṁ yas tv abhidrohoduḥsahaś cetanāpahaḥ
verses.text: O elevated demigods, at the time of death, severe, unbearable pain takes away the consciousness of all living entities who have accepted material bodies. Don’t you know about this pain?

verses.sanskrit: जिजीविषूणां जीवानामात्मा प्रेष्ठ इहेप्सित: ।क उत्सहेत तं दातुं भिक्षमाणाय विष्णवे ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: jijīviṣūṇāṁ jīvānāmātmā preṣṭha ihepsitaḥka utsaheta taṁ dātuṁbhikṣamāṇāya viṣṇave
verses.text: In this material world, every living entity is very much addicted to his material body. Struggling to keep his body forever, everyone tries to protect it by all means, even at the sacrifice of all his possessions. Therefore, who would be prepared to deliver his body to anyone, even if it were demanded by Lord Viṣṇu?

verses.sanskrit: श्रीदेवा ऊचु:किं नु तद् दुस्त्यजं ब्रह्मन् पुंसां भूतानुकम्पिनाम् ।भवद्विधानां महतां पुण्यश्लोकेड्यकर्मणाम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-devā ūcuḥkiṁ nu tad dustyajaṁ brahmanpuṁsāṁ bhūtānukampināmbhavad-vidhānāṁ mahatāṁpuṇya-ślokeḍya-karmaṇām
verses.text: The demigods replied: O exalted brāhmaṇa, pious persons like you, whose activities are praiseworthy, are very kind and affectionate to people in general. What can’t such pious souls give for the benefit of others? They can give everything, including their bodies.

verses.sanskrit: नूनं स्वार्थपरो लोको न वेद परसङ्कटम् ।यदि वेद न याचेत नेति नाह यदीश्वर: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: nūnaṁ svārtha-paro lokona veda para-saṅkaṭamyadi veda na yācetaneti nāha yad īśvaraḥ
verses.text: Those who are too self-interested beg something from others, not knowing of others’ pain. But if the beggar knew the difficulty of the giver, he would not ask for anything. Similarly, he who is able to give charity does not know the beggar’s difficulty, for otherwise he would not refuse to give the beggar anything he might want as charity.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीऋषिरुवाचधर्मं व: श्रोतुकामेन यूयं मे प्रत्युदाहृता: ।एष व: प्रियमात्मानं त्यजन्तं सन्त्यजाम्यहम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-ṛṣir uvācadharmaṁ vaḥ śrotu-kāmenayūyaṁ me pratyudāhṛtāḥeṣa vaḥ priyam ātmānaṁtyajantaṁ santyajāmy aham
verses.text: The great sage Dadhīci said: Just to hear from you about religious principles, I refused to offer my body at your request. Now, although my body is extremely dear to me, I must give it up for your better purposes since I know that it will leave me today or tomorrow.

verses.sanskrit: योऽध्रुवेणात्मना नाथा न धर्मं न यश: पुमान् ।ईहेत भूतदयया स शोच्य: स्थावरैरपि ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: yo ’dhruveṇātmanā nāthāna dharmaṁ na yaśaḥ pumānīheta bhūta-dayayāsa śocyaḥ sthāvarair api
verses.text: O demigods, one who has no compassion for humanity in its suffering and does not sacrifice his impermanent body for the higher causes of religious principles or eternal glory is certainly pitied even by the immovable beings.

verses.sanskrit: एतावानव्ययो धर्म: पुण्यश्लोकैरुपासित: ।यो भूतशोकहर्षाभ्यामात्मा शोचति हृष्यति ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: etāvān avyayo dharmaḥpuṇya-ślokair upāsitaḥyo bhūta-śoka-harṣābhyāmātmā śocati hṛṣyati
verses.text: If one is unhappy to see the distress of other living beings and happy to see their happiness, his religious principles are appreciated as imperishable by exalted persons who are considered pious and benevolent.

verses.sanskrit: अहो दैन्यमहो कष्टं पारक्यै: क्षणभङ्गुरै: ।यन्नोपकुर्यादस्वार्थैर्मर्त्य: स्वज्ञातिविग्रहै: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: aho dainyam aho kaṣṭaṁpārakyaiḥ kṣaṇa-bhaṅguraiḥyan nopakuryād asvārthairmartyaḥ sva-jñāti-vigrahaiḥ
verses.text: This body, which is eatable by jackals and dogs after death, does not actually do any good for me, the spirit soul. It is usable only for a short time and may perish at any moment. The body and its possessions, its riches and relatives, must all be engaged for the benefit of others, or else they will be sources of tribulation and misery.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाचएवं कृतव्यवसितो दध्यङ्‌ङाथर्वणस्तनुम् ।परे भगवति ब्रह्मण्यात्मानं सन्नयञ्जहौ ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvācaevaṁ kṛta-vyavasitodadhyaṅṅ ātharvaṇas tanumpare bhagavati brahmaṇyātmānaṁ sannayañ jahau
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Dadhīci Muni, the son of Atharvā, thus resolved to give his body to the service of the demigods. He placed himself, the spirit soul, at the lotus feet of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and in this way gave up his gross material body made of five elements.

verses.sanskrit: यताक्षासुमनोबुद्धिस्तत्त्वद‍ृग् ध्वस्तबन्धन: ।आस्थित: परमं योगं न देहं बुबुधे गतम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yatākṣāsu-mano-buddhistattva-dṛg dhvasta-bandhanaḥāsthitaḥ paramaṁ yogaṁna dehaṁ bubudhe gatam
verses.text: Dadhīci Muni controlled his senses, life force, mind and intelligence and became absorbed in trance. Thus he cut all his material bonds. He could not perceive how his material body became separated from his self.

verses.sanskrit: अथेन्द्रो वज्रमुद्यम्य निर्मितं विश्वकर्मणा ।मुने: शक्तिभिरुत्सिक्तो भगवत्तेजसान्वित: ॥ १३ ॥वृतो देवगणै: सर्वैर्गजेन्द्रोपर्यशोभत ।स्तूयमानो मुनिगणैस्त्रैलोक्यं हर्षयन्निव ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: athendro vajram udyamyanirmitaṁ viśvakarmaṇāmuneḥ śaktibhir utsiktobhagavat-tejasānvitaḥ
verses.text: Thereafter, King Indra very firmly took up the thunderbolt manufactured by Viśvakarmā from the bones of Dadhīci. Charged with the exalted power of Dadhīci Muni and enlightened by the power of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Indra rode on the back of his carrier, Airāvata, surrounded by all the demigods, while all the great sages offered him praise. Thus he shone very beautifully, pleasing the three worlds as he rode off to kill Vṛtrāsura.

verses.sanskrit: वृत्रमभ्यद्रवच्छत्रुमसुरानीकयूथपै: ।पर्यस्तमोजसाराजन् क्रुद्धो रुद्र इवान्तकम् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: vṛtram abhyadravac chatrumasurānīka-yūthapaiḥparyastam ojasā rājankruddho rudra ivāntakam
verses.text: My dear King Parīkṣit, as Rudra, being very angry at Antaka [Yamarāja] had formerly run toward Antaka to kill him, Indra angrily and with great force attacked Vṛtrāsura, who was surrounded by the leaders of the demoniac armies.

verses.sanskrit: तत: सुराणामसुरै रण: परमदारुण: ।त्रेतामुखेनर्मदायामभवत्प्रथमे युगे ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ surāṇām asurairaṇaḥ parama-dāruṇaḥtretā-mukhe narmadāyāmabhavat prathame yuge
verses.text: Thereafter, at the end of Satya-yuga and the beginning of Tretā-yuga, a fierce battle took place between the demigods and the demons on the bank of the Narmadā.

verses.sanskrit: रुद्रैर्वसुभिरादित्यैरश्विभ्यां पितृवह्निभि: ।मरुद्भ‍िर्ऋभुभि: साध्यैर्विश्वेदेवैर्मरुत्पतिम् ॥ १७ ॥द‍ृष्ट्वा वज्रधरं शक्रं रोचमानं स्वया श्रिया ।नामृष्यन्नसुरा राजन्मृधे वृत्रपुर:सरा: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: rudrair vasubhir ādityairaśvibhyāṁ pitṛ-vahnibhiḥmarudbhir ṛbhubhiḥ sādhyairviśvedevair marut-patim
verses.text: O King, when all the asuras came onto the battlefield, headed by Vṛtrāsura, they saw King Indra carrying the thunderbolt and surrounded by the Rudras, Vasus, Ādityas, Aśvinī-kumāras, Pitās, Vahnis, Maruts, Ṛbhus, Sādhyas and Viśvadevas. Surrounded by his company, Indra shone so brightly that his effulgence was intolerable to the demons.

verses.sanskrit: नमुचि: शम्बरोऽनर्वा द्विमूर्धा ऋषभोऽसुर: ।हयग्रीव: शङ्कुशिरा विप्रचित्तिरयोमुख: ॥ १९ ॥पुलोमा वृषपर्वा च प्रहेतिर्हेतिरुत्कल: ।दैतेया दानवा यक्षा रक्षांसि च सहस्रश: ॥ २० ॥सुमालिमालिप्रमुखा: कार्तस्वरपरिच्छदा: ।प्रतिषिध्येन्द्रसेनाग्रं मृत्योरपि दुरासदम् ॥ २१ ॥अभ्यर्दयन्नसम्भ्रान्ता: सिंहनादेन दुर्मदा: ।गदाभि: परिघैर्बाणै: प्रासमुद्गरतोमरै: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: namuciḥ śambaro ’narvādvimūrdhā ṛṣabho ’suraḥhayagrīvaḥ śaṅkuśirāvipracittir ayomukhaḥ
verses.text: Many hundreds and thousands of demons, demi-demons, Yakṣas, Rākṣasas [man-eaters] and others, headed by Sumāli and Māli, resisted the armies of King Indra, which even death personified cannot easily overcome. Among the demons were Namuci, Śambara, Anarvā, Dvimūrdhā, Ṛṣabha, Asura, Hayagrīva, Śaṅkuśirā, Vipracitti, Ayomukha, Pulomā, Vṛṣaparvā, Praheti, Heti and Utkala. Roaring tumultuously and fearlessly like lions, these invincible demons, all dressed in golden ornaments, gave pain to the demigods with weapons like clubs, bludgeons, arrows, barbed darts, mallets and lances.

verses.sanskrit: शूलै: परश्वधै: खड्‌गै: शतघ्नीभिर्भुशुण्डिभि: ।सर्वतोऽवाकिरन् शस्त्रैरस्त्रैश्च विबुधर्षभान् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śūlaiḥ paraśvadhaiḥ khaḍgaiḥśataghnībhir bhuśuṇḍibhiḥsarvato ’vākiran śastrairastraiś ca vibudharṣabhān
verses.text: Armed with lances, tridents, axes, swords and other weapons like śataghnīs and bhuśuṇḍis, the demons attacked from different directions and scattered all the chiefs of the demigod armies.

verses.sanskrit: न तेऽद‍ृश्यन्त सञ्छन्ना: शरजालै: समन्तत: ।पुङ्खानुपुङ्खपतितैर्ज्योतींषीव नभोघनै: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: na te ’dṛśyanta sañchannāḥśara-jālaiḥ samantataḥpuṅkhānupuṅkha-patitairjyotīṁṣīva nabho-ghanaiḥ
verses.text: As the stars in the sky cannot be seen when covered by dense clouds, the demigods, being completely covered by networks of arrows falling upon them one after another, could not be seen.

verses.sanskrit: न ते शस्त्रास्त्रवर्षौघा ह्यासेदु: सुरसैनिकान् ।छिन्ना: सिद्धपथे देवैर्लघुहस्तै: सहस्रधा ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: na te śastrāstra-varṣaughāhy āseduḥ sura-sainikānchinnāḥ siddha-pathe devairlaghu-hastaiḥ sahasradhā
verses.text: The showers of various weapons and arrows released to kill the soldiers of the demigods did not reach them because the demigods, acting quickly, cut the weapons into thousands of pieces in the sky.

verses.sanskrit: अथ क्षीणास्त्रशस्त्रौघा गिरिश‍ृङ्गद्रुमोपलै: ।अभ्यवर्षन् सुरबलं चिच्छिदुस्तांश्च पूर्ववत् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: atha kṣīṇāstra-śastraughāgiri-śṛṅga-drumopalaiḥabhyavarṣan sura-balaṁcicchidus tāṁś ca pūrvavat
verses.text: As their weapons and mantras decreased, the demons began showering mountain peaks, trees and stones upon the demigod soldiers, but the demigods were so powerful and expert that they nullified all these weapons by breaking them to pieces in the sky as before.

verses.sanskrit: तानक्षतान् स्वस्तिमतो निशाम्यशस्त्रास्त्रपूगैरथ वृत्रनाथा: ।द्रुमैर्द‍ृषद्भ‍िर्विविधाद्रिश‍ृङ्गैरविक्षतांस्तत्रसुरिन्द्रसैनिकान् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tān akṣatān svastimato niśāmyaśastrāstra-pūgair atha vṛtra-nāthāḥdrumair dṛṣadbhir vividhādri-śṛṅgairavikṣatāṁs tatrasur indra-sainikān
verses.text: When the soldiers of the demons, commanded by Vṛtrāsura, saw that the soldiers of King Indra were quite well, having not been injured at all by their volleys of weapons, not even by the trees, stones and mountain peaks, the demons were very much afraid.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे प्रयासा अभवन् विमोघा:कृता: कृता देवगणेषु दैत्यै: ।कृष्णानुकूलेषु यथा महत्सुक्षुद्रै: प्रयुक्ता ऊषती रूक्षवाच: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarve prayāsā abhavan vimoghāḥkṛtāḥ kṛtā deva-gaṇeṣu daityaiḥkṛṣṇānukūleṣu yathā mahatsukṣudraiḥ prayuktā ūṣatī rūkṣa-vācaḥ
verses.text: When insignificant persons use rough words to cast false, angry accusations against saintly persons, their fruitless words do not disturb the great personalities. Similarly, all the efforts of the demons against the demigods, who were favorably situated under the protection of Kṛṣṇa, were futile.

verses.sanskrit: ते स्वप्रयासं वितथं निरीक्ष्यहरावभक्ता हतयुद्धदर्पा: ।पलायनायाजिमुखे विसृज्यपतिं मनस्ते दधुरात्तसारा: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: te sva-prayāsaṁ vitathaṁ nirīkṣyaharāv abhaktā hata-yuddha-darpāḥpalāyanāyāji-mukhe visṛjyapatiṁ manas te dadhur ātta-sārāḥ
verses.text: The asuras, who are never devotees of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, lost their pride in fighting when they found all their endeavors futile. Leaving aside their leader even in the very beginning of the fight, they decided to flee because all their prowess had been taken away by the enemy.

verses.sanskrit: वृत्रोऽसुरांस्ताननुगान् मनस्वीप्रधावत: प्रेक्ष्य बभाष एतत् ।पलायितं प्रेक्ष्य बलं च भग्नंभयेन तीव्रेण विहस्य वीर: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: vṛtro ’surāṁs tān anugān manasvīpradhāvataḥ prekṣya babhāṣa etatpalāyitaṁ prekṣya balaṁ ca bhagnaṁbhayena tīvreṇa vihasya vīraḥ
verses.text: Seeing his army broken and all the asuras, even those known as great heroes, fleeing the battlefield out of intense fear, Vṛtrāsura, who was truly a great-minded hero, smiled and spoke the following words.

verses.sanskrit: कालोपपन्नां रुचिरां मनस्विनांजगाद वाचं पुरुषप्रवीर: ।हे विप्रचित्ते नमुचे पुलोमन्मयानर्वञ्छम्बर मे श‍ृणुध्वम् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: kālopapannāṁ rucirāṁ manasvināṁjagāda vācaṁ puruṣa-pravīraḥhe vipracitte namuce pulomanmayānarvañ chambara me śṛṇudhvam
verses.text: According to his position and the time and circumstances, Vṛtrāsura, the hero among heroes, spoke words that were much to be appreciated by thoughtful men. He called to the heroes of the demons, “O Vipracitti! O Namuci! O Pulomā! O Maya, Anarvā and Śambara! Please hear me and do not flee.”

verses.sanskrit: जातस्य मृत्युर्ध्रुव एव सर्वत:प्रतिक्रिया यस्य न चेह क्लृप्ता ।लोको यशश्चाथ ततो यदि ह्यमुंको नाम मृत्युं न वृणीत युक्तम् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: jātasya mṛtyur dhruva eva sarvataḥpratikriyā yasya na ceha kḷptāloko yaśaś cātha tato yadi hy amuṁko nāma mṛtyuṁ na vṛṇīta yuktam
verses.text: Vṛtrāsura said: All living entities who have taken birth in this material world must die. Surely, no one in this world has found any means to be saved from death. Even providence has not provided a means to escape it. Under the circumstances, death being inevitable, if one can gain promotion to the higher planetary systems and be always celebrated here by dying a suitable death, what man will not accept such a glorious death?

verses.sanskrit: द्वौ सम्मताविह मृत्यू दुरापौयद् ब्रह्मसन्धारणया जितासु: ।कलेवरं योगरतो विजह्याद्यदग्रणीर्वीरशयेऽनिवृत्त: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: dvau sammatāv iha mṛtyū durāpauyad brahma-sandhāraṇayā jitāsuḥkalevaraṁ yoga-rato vijahyādyad agraṇīr vīra-śaye ’nivṛttaḥ
verses.text: There are two ways to meet a glorious death, and both are very rare. One is to die after performing mystic yoga, especially bhakti-yoga, by which one can control the mind and living force and die absorbed in thought of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The second is to die on the battlefield, leading the army and never showing one’s back. These two kinds of death are recommended in the śāstra as glorious.
